ops.title: DEPLOYMENT TAILNET IS SHARED, NOT DEDICATED (tail71af5d.ts.net, yasyfm@gmail.com): it hosts other production nodes — tag:sprite, tag:gcp, tag:zo, tag:modal — plus GCP route autoApprovers, svc:n8n, mullvad/funnel/drive nodeAttrs, and a default ALLOW-ALL acls rule. The repo's tailnet/policy.hujson (default-deny, yclaw-only) and the (now-removed) tailscale-acl.yml workflow assumed a DEDICATED tailnet; applying/replacing with that policy would deauthorize the other nodes and cut the tailnet. Resolution: yclaw tagOwners (hermes/metal/bluebubbles) + an admin-SSH rule were added ADDITIVELY to the live ACL via the Tailscale API (backup at ~/yclaw-tailscale-acl-backup.hujson). CONSEQUENCE: the repo's H3/H4 default-deny east-west lockdown is NOT in effect — yclaw nodes are reachable per the allow-all ACL; metal's service ports stay pf-gated to the tailnet CGNAT but to ALL tailnet nodes, not hermes-only. Full hermes-only lockdown would require redesigning the whole tailnet ACL (the user's other nodes need explicit grants).
